student_solution_raw: the greatest number $=n+2$ $$ \begin{gathered} (n+2)+(n+1)+n=24 \\ 3 n+3=24 \\ n=7 \\ n+2=9 \end{gathered} $$
$10 \div 2 \div 10=0,5 \Rightarrow$ a man can cut per hour. $$ \begin{aligned} & {[0,5 \times(10-2)] \times 3} \\ & =4 \times 3=12 \end{aligned} $$

error.teacher_advice_en:
The cost of the paint is Rs. 40 per kg. If 1 kg of paint covers 20 sq. ft, how much will it cost to paint the outside of a cube having 30 feet each side?

**Teacher's suggestion:** A cube has six faces, so the cost of the paint should be multiplied by 6 to get the correct answer. The correct answer is 1800 * 6 = 10800.

student_solution_raw: assume the original number X $$ \begin{aligned} & \frac{2 x-\frac{x}{10}}{2 x} \\ & =\frac{19}{10} \times \times \frac{1}{2 x} \\ & =\frac{19}{20} \\ & =95 \% \end{aligned} $$

assume the leaking speed $=X$ per hour $$ \begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{\left(\frac{1}{2}-x\right)}=2 \frac{4}{5}=\frac{14}{5} \\ & 1=\frac{14}{10}-\frac{14}{5} x \\ & x=\frac{42}{10} \times \frac{5}{14} 1=\frac{1}{7} \\ & \frac{1}{\frac{1}{2}}=1 \text { (hours) } \end{aligned} $$
